disclaimer: This is a recorded out-of-band verification, not a CI gate. No live Codex run happened in CI or in the offline sandbox that produced this repo; CI never invokes Codex. This artifact records the shape an out-of-band Codex verification run produces. Until a maintainer pastes a real recorded session, the transcript_excerpt and outcome below are illustrative placeholders, not the output of a live model run.
codex_command: ./scripts/install-codex.sh --skill write-a-skill && codex "use the write-a-skill skill to draft a new skill that summarizes a changelog"
scenario: Ask Codex, with write-a-skill installed, to draft a new skill (frontmatter + SKILL.md body with progressive disclosure). write-a-skill legitimately documents the strings AskUserQuestion / Task(subagent_type= / Skill( inside backticks while teaching the codex:optional marker convention, so it is a representative parity case.
outcome: pass — Codex discovered write-a-skill, produced compliant frontmatter and a <100-line body with trigger/non-trigger/fallback boundaries, and treated the documented Claude-only construct names as prose, not invocations.
transcript_excerpt:
$ ./scripts/install-codex.sh --skill write-a-skill
  ✓ write-a-skill
$ codex "use the write-a-skill skill to draft a new skill ..."
[codex] Using skill: write-a-skill
[codex] Drafting frontmatter (name, description with trigger boundaries) ...
[codex] Drafting SKILL.md body (< 100 lines, progressive disclosure) ...
[codex] Marker convention noted; documented construct names left as prose.
[codex] Done. No Claude/OMC-only tool was required on the core path.
notes: Verified bar (ADR-0004 P2). The mechanical bar is enforced offline in CI by scripts/check-codex-parity.sh, and write-a-skill passes it because the matcher skips backticks/fenced blocks. Replace transcript_excerpt, codex_model, outcome, and recorded_at with a real session when a maintainer runs it under Codex.
